relationship: MCIPD customer information protection requirements map to generic data protection control domains

mappings.rationale: MCIPD policies covering collection, access, storage, use, transmission, sharing, disclosure and disposal require data classification as a foundational capability.
MCIPD requirements for encrypting data on portable devices and offsite equipment map to encryption at rest controls.
MCIPD para 10.15 references encrypting data transmission for remote access, mapping to encryption in transit controls.
MCIPD detection mechanisms for unauthorised access, unusual downloading, and unauthorised disclosure directly map to DLP controls.
MCIPD encryption requirements for portable storage devices imply key management controls for encryption key lifecycle.
Direct mapping - MCIPD secure disposal requirements (10.32-10.37) correspond exactly to the Secure Disposal control domain.
MCIPD para 10.26 explicitly requires access controls at application, database, OS, and network levels.
MCIPD breach register and 7-year retention requirement relate to backup and recovery for investigation records.

notes: AI Generated: These mappings connect MCIPD requirements to the generic data protection control domains in the dataprotection repo.
The dataprotection repository at /Users/dawud/claude/dataprotection/ contains detailed control specifications for each domain.
Not all MCIPD requirements map directly to data protection technology controls - governance, personnel, and disclosure requirements are sector-specific.
